Thought 3: The camera showing no movement at the time of the murder is critical. Since the cabin is isolated with only one entrance/exit, and the camera monitors that point, it means no one entered or left at the exact time of the murder. However, this doesn't necessarily mean no one was already inside. Santiago had invited Orville and told him "I will be alone," which might imply Orville was expected to come but not necessarily that he was the only one there. Troy was found inside the cabin later, but we don't know if he was there during the murder.

Thought 4: Let me consider the implications of Santiago's statement to Orville: "I will be alone at the cabin." This could be a warning or a setup. Perhaps Santiago knew Orville was coming and wanted to emphasize his solitude to lure him? Or it might be unrelated. Also, Troy is already in the cabin when Winston visits, but we need to know if Troy was present during the murder.
